dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess of love, always one step ahead of me. How many mortals do you suppose would seek me out to bless their wedding vows, had Aphrodite not already worked her charms on them? It seems our strengths go hand in hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Golden Apollo already offered you his aid, has he now? Always so eager to please. I find his constant doting bothersome... but doubtless you're more tolerant than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Most all of us have a taste for violence, girl, but my son Ares takes it to a quite uncomfortable extreme. He may be useful in such times, but we mustn't let him get too excited.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We've sent young Artemis near to the border of your realm and ours. We require some additional awareness there, and you likely could benefit from a bit of covering fire. Sometimes I miss wielding a bow myself...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We've wise Athena holding back the front but probably could use a score of others equally as capable. She has the Titan's raiders running scared, but... even she can't always stop them all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem adept at keeping distance from your enemies. I once excelled at this myself, although I favored an arrow through the heart over magick, which I found rather unreliable. But, whatever works for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, the blessings that my kin and I bestow, why... they're a bit like children, I suppose. You don't always get the kind you want, but if you're clever, you can make them work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I always wanted a little girl, you know? But I got Ares. Hephaestus. Eugh! Zeus' girls Athena and Artemis stay well afield of me, and Aphrodite, oh, she does what she wants. Some family we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Our great big family, why, we would have been just a small fraction of the size, if not for my husband's escapades! So many delightful children in my life as a result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ever think of having children of your own, my dear? If so, perhaps you're thinking twice after all this. But we shall need another generation soon enough! And it may as well come from good stock.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, girl, this world is ours by right. The Fates themselves decided it! Now Chronos seeks to overthrow our reign? He already had his opportunity to rule. There is no going back to how things were.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan Chronos is no father of mine. But your father sprang from him, as did my husband, and Poseidon as well. So, now we're all tied up in this little family affair!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that I could put another arrow in between the eyes of Chronos again myself, but alas, you're closer and I'm stuck. Kill him for me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: This matter with Chronos, it may seem unconscionable that a parent could resent their own children. But let me tell you, girl, there are moments when I almost sympathize.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I always knew Chronos would return. Zeus kept saying I was fearful over nothing! We buried the old wretch in the depths of Tartarus, after all. But he's a deathless Titan. Time, it seems, slipped through our fingers...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Do find a mate who pays you proper compliments, girl! My Lord Husband, why, he calls me ox-eyed Lady Hera now and then! Says I've the big, beautiful eyes of a large bovine, ever the romantic that he is!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I never used to wear the same gown for two successive nights, let alone this long. But now we've no such time for pageantry. I miss those outfits, shimmering like stars! White-armed Hera I was, ever since my wedding day...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You're a witch. You must dabble in curses, yes? I've done a few of my own... one for this Nymph who talked too much, others for more-unspeakable acts. We must assert ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Cold vengeance... I can feel it from afar. I suppose my sister Demeter has more at stake in this than anybody on this mountaintop. Our enemies shall rue having crossed her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The other night, given the opportunity, you sided with my kin, forgoing my Boon when I first generously offered it. Whilst that little scuffle is behind us, I do not appreciate any such slights. I trust I've made myself quite clear?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Expect no aid from Dionysus, who must be in a stupor somewhere, unless your idea of aid lies at the bottom of a drinking-cup. Though, come to think, that may not be the worst idea of late...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: No surprise that Strife Incarnate recently decided to show up, as she is wont to do. Eris made good sport of us before! Must think we're fools and easy prey. You'll correct her, yes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: One thing I've learned, my dear, is that forever is a very long time. It seems to me that nothing truly lasts forever anymore. Perhaps that is why we find ourselves in this particular predicament.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: This is hardly the first time I've been pressed to intervene on your side of the family's behalf. I've stood my ground! We cannot keep getting involved in each other's affairs. Yet, if you've come to our defense, you're owed an exception...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen how we don't always get along, but once you've lived with this lot long enough, you'll be right there with us. Tolerance becomes a practiced skill! But we do love each other, by and large...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are gone, then, yes? According to Hermes? Quite the unfortunate turn of events if true. Well, if Chronos is bent on vengeance, it only makes sense that he would take it out on them, not just on us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm to believe that Chronos truly doesn't know where he left the Three Fates? The weavers of destiny themselves, misplaced and forgotten like some trinkets! They're probably enjoying being rid of all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen the Mourning Fields within your Underworld, yes? A solid portion of the residents there are due to failed marriages... most typically their own. I bless each matrimony that I can, but it can only do so much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, your poor father. You must have been the jewel in his eye, only to have been ripped away! He would have been so very proud. Well, perhaps not proud, as such. He was never big on taking pride in anything, now that I think on it. I'm proud of you, at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Witch of the Crossroads evidently raised you well! The younger generation here, they lack in character, and their manners oft leave much to be desired. Almost as though my generation did not set the best example!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, at least Hephaestus is attempting to be of use! You'll have to excuse my sheltered son, girl. He'd much prefer to clang ceaselessly upon his forge than to be a part of polite society.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My soft-spoken son Hephaestus thinks I can be rather too unkind. Perhaps he's right! What, you think my mother showered me with love? Neither you yours. This is a harsh world, girl. No one asks to be born into it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Word reached me you've encountered mighty Heracles! Big, thick-headed fellow? You're not to aid him in his labors, do you understand? He's in the middle of what we Olympians like to call a journey of self-discovery.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: That Heracles is Zeus' lad, you know. Glory of Hera! I should be honored. He's just another mouth for me to feed, and one with quite an appetite, at that. But, I'll keep him busy, sure! We need all the help we can get.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd tell us if Heracles wasn't living up to expectations, yes? Confidentially, we're thinking once things finally settle down, we may invite him to join us on a more-permanent basis. He's labored enough, and we could use the muscle.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Where is that Hermes when you need him, have you seen him lately, girl? Always flitting about, impossible to pin down. Eugh. What news could there be to deliver at a time like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to see that you're agreeable with Hestia, my dear. Her temper and my own don't often mesh, but I respect her wisdom, and her power to simply burn everything down if and when necessary.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You always were one of the best hopes that we had in this, my dear. But frankly, I had little faith we would prevail, with you or without. So for this once, I'm pleased to have been wrong, and shall not soon forget all that you've done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My sisters Demeter and Hestia and I, our father was the Titan called Hyperion. Father by technicality at least. You don't hear much about him anymore! We should have dealt with Chronos just the same back when we had the chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, if it isn't my Iridescent Fan, illuminated even in the night! The easier with which to spot you wheresoever you may be. Should you require my immediate assistance, merely wave it about a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: That Iridescent Fan I offered you has clearly been of more than decorative use! I had a feeling that you'd take to it, my dear. Never hesitate to give it a shake if you need me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: That last resort, which we would have used against Typhon, had you not found more of a targeted solution... merely something Hephaestus has been working on, halfheartedly. Would have cost my son his favored forge, and more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I realize this may seem odd coming from me, my dear, but from one goddess to another: Do not rush into marriage! As much as it may seem a wondrous idea. Consider my example; and I'm the blasted goddess of marriage, herself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah! There you are again, my dear! Pity to have only met in circumstances such as these. Yet families all go through their ups and downs, yes? Let me help you help us all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: To level with you, girl, I don't know that you've got what it takes to thwart the Titan. But, what choice do we have at this point? I'm willing to give you the benefit of the doubt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We had an obligation to your father here. Each mortal death adds to the population of the Underworld, after all! I tried to do my blasted part to curb the flow a bit. Have you any idea how onerous it is for mortals to give birth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: If ever you wondered whence came all the wretches in your Underworld, there are such mortals who see it as our responsibility to protect them! Can you believe it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I hardly blame them but some mortals have grown skeptical about our governance. They have this newfangled idea of luck... strokes of fortune from thin air! Sheer mindless happenstance, not the work of gods or the Three Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, mortalkind must take us for a pack of bickering fools, though they'd never admit it. We shall have to save face with them somehow. I'll ask that Demeter ensure more times of plenty are to come, and such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, I can barely reach you as it is within that murk, and you seem even farther away now. The very outskirts of your father's realm, is it? That's... not the customary path into the Underworld....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Traveling this way again, are you, my dear? Doubtless to put the nonsensical theories of your Titan grandfather to the test! If you detect even a whiff of lies, do tell. Even if we're to be merciful, we still mustn't be fools.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen the state our poor mountain is in. The embarrassing inventions of Hephaestus run as rampant as our enemies! Some of us remain cooped up in the Palace of Zeus away from it all. Quite lovely here at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Something about this night feels very odd; and in my experience, such premonitions are never to be ignored. Perhaps the strange forces at work are merely due to your invocations! Just... do be careful, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I know something is going on tonight, but cannot say exactly what. Perhaps it's that Witch of the Crossroads, conjuring to our collective benefit. Or your own handiwork?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to have finally met in a more-proper way, my dear! You bought this mountain of ours a bit of extra time, so we are bolstering our defenses as best we can... thus I'm beseeching you to hurry back!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk, you never even got to see the Palace proper, my dear. It's not presentable anyhow. Perhaps by the time your father's House is back in order, ours shall be as well! I quite insist you visit at that point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You know the Twilight Curse, oh I can tell. The power to change a form into another! A favorite of mine, and one small step removed from shapeshifting oneself. The possibilities are limitless, really.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Confronted Polyphemus, have you, then? That is the sort of help Chronos employs. A hollow fool! But strong, admittedly. No sneaking past him, I'm afraid. We've tried....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Had I but known that stout Poseidon went out of his way for you earlier, I would have got here even sooner. The sea is unreliable, whilst my bond is forever. Figuratively, at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: First Chronos and now Prometheus are back to haunt us, all because we failed to get rid of them back when we had a chance. Who next, I wonder? You witches really need to come up with a means to dispense with immortals for good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My Lord Husband and Prometheus are not on, shall we say, the best of terms. And that Titan has made clear what he thinks of us goddesses and gods. Yet, now we're tolerating him, to clean the mess he helped create? Absurd...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Aphrodite has quite gleefully informed me that you and certain friends of yours have grown quite close. Well if one thing leads to another and you decide to make a serious commitment, I should be only too pleased to oblige!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: A fine girl such as yourself must have suitors! Just don't be quick to wed if you can help it, yes? Mortals like to tie themselves down for the remainders of their lives but often barely manage it. And their lifetimes are nothing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We might not care for it but there's a certain power to being underestimated constantly. Right, girl? Let them think what they want; my blessings aren't merely for couples about to tie the knot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My husband Zeus and I have spirited debates regarding how you may ensure our victory. He is too proud to seek your help here on our mountaintop, whilst I would welcome it with open arms, so... come along!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, this has been a real journey, hasn't it, my dear? I may be briefer with you hence, as other matters increasingly demand my attention. Though, you'll have my blessings for as long as you require.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I saw a flicker in the sky before, as though the moonlight shifted for a moment. But now it's like that light is shining from you. You drew Selene down! She would not answer such a call from most.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: This night has left me feeling ill-at-ease, for many of our enemies are growing rather bold. Mortals experience fear when their lives are at risk, but this cannot be that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: That fearsome feeling in the air, so then it is your doing, isn't it, girl? I fail to see how riling up our enemies shall do us any good. Now you go deal with them at once!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you know Night Bloom, of course. A means to raise the dead for a little while. Easier than raising live children, honestly... and significantly quicker, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome, my dear, to our world's surface! First time? It's a vast place! All sorts of different climates, mortal cultures, and the like. So don't let your first impression shake your faith; it isn't always such a disaster!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Mortals complained their city of Ephyra was forsaken by the gods, yet here we are! Ungrateful wretches. Not had many weddings of late. Not many funerals either... though, lots of death, destruction, and the like!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: There are many potent reagents growing on the surface here, if you know where to look! Mere roots and flowers to the untrained eye, but in your hands, or better, in your cauldron? They'll have you working magick like never before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: They said you weren't cut out for being aboveground! Some Underworld curse holding you back... I'd not believed the hearsay for a moment, and sure enough. Perhaps they merely fear your potential.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: There was a recent rumbling about Olympus, which caused us some concern. A real rumbling, I mean! Even Poseidon was surprised at all the shaking. What, does Chronos mean to topple the entire mountain?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, good girl, to have turned your attention to the Underworld at last! It's so humiliating all that's happened to us here. Least we can do is clean up after ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a dreadful fleet that sails our way... when last I witnessed one of such magnitude, the ships were sailing in my honor, rather than against my home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: It's blasted Typhon, girl! The worst monstrosity you can imagine. Chronos up and decided to wake him from his deathly slumber, and pointed him towards us! So what if we cannot die? Things can still be very bad for us...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You truly rid us of that blasted Typhon. Never have we faced such an overwhelming threat, and all we could do was keep him at bay! Now we have the upper hand again because of you, my dear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Such power you possess, my dear... whatever it is you did to Typhon, that's a secret to be guarded well. We could keep it for you! Our vaults have never once been breached. Think on it, yes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Old Typhon suffered a bit of a spill last night but already is beginning to stir, Hermes reports. His target clearly is our summit... if only he were as easy to thwart as to predict.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Headed down to check on your mother and father, my dear? The House of Hades probably could use your touch. If only my own children would be so considerate. Though, I suppose they are still aiding you...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I know something of witchcraft myself, girl. A subtle art, and therein lies its strength. Easy to disbelieve, or to dismiss as mere coincidence. But true power need not assert itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah! I see that my distinguished husband has already been in touch. Well, just in case he doesn't make the sparks fly in quite the manner you expect, I have something for you of my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Again, Lord Zeus has reached you first, has he? Heh. The noble Son of Chronos and his lightning speed! You can't imagine just how quickly he flees from me, sometimes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: For all his foibles, my husband makes a dangerous adversary. Why else do you think we've ruled for however many aeons it's been? Zeus doubtless has his enemies, but few are brave or misguided enough to let on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Zeus and I, we've known each other for a time. A regular old married couple we are! And yet such awful things are often said of us. I try to brush it off! No couple is beyond reproach; certainly, not one that's survived as long as we.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not taking any chances, girl. For instance, we are not above having our blessings transported like common parcels, if in so doing, we ensure your victory and ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Admit it, girl! You would have taken any sort of blessing here and now, whether from me or any of my kin. Well as the Fates would have it, you're getting the very best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: To think that the blessings of the Queen of Olympus could be fetched from some unassuming sack! Whatever damage this mountain has sustained, our pride has suffered worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: A blessing for you, and a curse for our enemies, my dear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: How odd that this conflict tearing us apart is bringing us closer together, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos is an old fool whose time has long since passed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely one amongst my blessings ought to suit you well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Show no weakness, show no mercy, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Just look at us, girl! Our family's a mess, but we're making it work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Hera's here for you, girl; and soon, all your enemies shall wish that they were never born.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Great Aunt's back, my dear, so let's have ourselves an interesting night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing like jointly annihilating our enemies to bring the family closer together, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Surprise, surprise, you're once again where I expected you to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, girl, let's just have a look at you and see what we can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps our family deserves all this, though we'll keep fighting back against it nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: To think all we needed was a perilous existential threat to unite this family, for once!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Chin up, girl! Things could probably end up much worse than they are now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family may be cursed by the Fates themselves, but we do know how to fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We've so many enemies, I cannot curse them all! Though I can bless you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Care for a little power over life itself? Or death. I've got that, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I confess I'm beginning to enjoy these little outings that we share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Raised in the Underworld, yet you've more poise and grace than the lot of them up here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: More and more it seems you're just about the only one I can depend on lately, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm in a bit of a foul mood right now, so quickly choose, and go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: This little tradition of ours has become something I look forward to, dear girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates denied me any daughters, but at least they brought me you, my dear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Life has its wretched spans every so often, so the better times stand out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We on Olympus take our vengeance very personally, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that some others that I knew had even but a shred of your commitment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: It simply is our lot to have to shoulder more than our fair share of any burden, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You've no idea how long it's been since I've depended upon anybody of our line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Our enemy has scorned us and we'll make him rue the very day, won't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I've enough power vested in me that I'm pleased to spare you some of it for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You have more royal bearing than I would have thought...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon this whole matter shall be put to bed, but till then, we persevere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: For all your patience and commitment, I'd say you're ready to be wed!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Our mountain's still an utter mess, if you don't mind having to clean up a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Palace necessarily remains off limits, though you're free to make the trip.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We still cannot get to your Underworld's depths, if you could keep an eye on things?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Do go make certain your lord father and dear mother still are carrying on well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us stay vigilant as we rebuild, lest we make the same missteps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: For all the family squabbles that we have, we mustn't let them get like this again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on then, girl, and make all our enemies rue the day they were born.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The family stands united in our cause... In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to make your acquaintance finally, my dear! Already had enough of the surface, yes? Heh, well I promised the family that once you demonstrated your resolve to us, I'd take you under my wing. And I always keep my vows.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The family stands united in our cause... In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased to make your acquaintance finally, my dear! Welcome to our world's surface, such as it is. I promised the family that once you demonstrated your resolve to us, then I'd take you under my wing... and, I always keep my vows.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The family stands united in our cause... In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there she is! Already had enough of the surface, yes? Heh, well I promised the family that once you demonstrated your resolve to us, I'd take you under my wing. And I always keep my vows.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The family stands united in our cause... In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there you are, my dear! Welcome to our world's surface, such as it is. I promised the family that once you demonstrated your resolve to us, then I'd take you under my wing... and, I always keep my vows.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Queen Hera, out of all the matters you could be attending to, you've chosen to aid me. I am honored, and would honor you in kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Is there a wedding date that I should know about or are you merely being generous with me? In any case, when I am offered to, there's always a catch. So, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Great Queen of Olympus! I am humbled to have earned your favor, and will do everything I can to honor you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Unbound by wedlock yet you praise me thus? I must say you are full of surprises, my dear. Or potentially just very ignorant. Or both!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This golden Nectar is nothing in comparison to your splendor, Queen Hera; but it's the very best we have where I am from. To your eternal beauty, grace, and wisdom!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You needn't be a flatterer, you know? I aid you not from love but from necessity. If there's a lesson I can impart, it's that you ought to keep your guard up at all times.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To the Queen of Olympus! Long may you reign, and may that reign be one of great prosperity and peace, both for your devoted family and subjects, and for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on, girl. We each know I'm not really your Queen. You're not even an Olympian, so why don't you drop the act? Or if it's not an act, wise up. This is a complicated world.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Queen Hera, though these exchanges between us must necessarily be brief, I'm grateful for each moment you can spare. I know your family needs you, and Olympus needs you... and your devotion to each is abundantly clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, my dear, if you find no future to be had in your witchcraft, you could always take up theatrics! For I very nearly believed your every word. Honesty is not our family's trade, but! I commend your effort anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You may not trust me at my word, O Queen; but with this offering of Nectar you shall find I bear no falsehoods or duplicitous intent. You're not just royalty to me... you're my Great Aunt. And as glorious as I could have imagined.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really serious, aren't you? Look, girl. You don't seem to understand what it's like on this mountain. There is no margin for error here; no room to slip! Although perhaps you've had a taste of that sort of pressure for yourself...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for holding our family together through the most trying of times, Queen Hera! And for having faith in me to make a difference. May the sweetness of this Ambrosia start to dull the memory of our bitter struggles.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: That's very kind of you, my dear. But my memory is long and sharp, and I'd not let it dull on purpose or otherwise. Our memories embolden us, and keep us from repeating our mistakes. Thank the Fates I was not mistaken about you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: How about we break ourselves out of this rut, girl, so here! This is the best I've got for you for now. Do make the most of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My mood darkens again, my dear, although it makes me rather generous, I think. Here, then! The finest of my blessings. Go on, and tie some knots!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We've a long-standing partnership with your good boatman there. I bring mortals into being and marry them off after a bit, he takes them yonder to your realm! Or used to, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Wealth cannot be measured in mere coin! The very idea is absurd; yet, there you are, paying for goods and services as though Chronos has already prevailed!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We cannot always reach you in those depths, though your boatman seems to have no trouble at all. Keeps to himself, fends for himself... I quite like him!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: At least this was a shorter delivery for your trusted boatman, compared with having to drag our Boons all the way into the Underworld. Although, he probably prefers it there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! You're a frightful mess, if you'll forgive my saying, girl! I can't help you when you're practically dead! At least, not very much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: What happened to all your lifeblood? That's precious stuff! Yet you've gone spilling it about, then have the nerve to thus present yourself to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: However much you've trained, it evidently was not nearly enough, if you've already taken such a beating before I can even begin to protect you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Not having a good night...? Your injuries will heal eventually, my dear. But, I can't make any promises about your pride.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgiveness is like a precious healing ointment, to be applied sparingly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine! That shall be all, I think. This type of squabble is beneath us both.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You wish to test me, I shall test you back, my dear. Please, do remember that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough already! Here's a blessing that you could have had without a fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Your struggles only entertain me for so long. Begone already, girl.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at us, bickering like an old married couple! What say we move on?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Consider yourself reprimanded, my dear. Know that you got off lightly this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, we've made enough enemies already that we hardly need more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We're royalty, my dear. We ought behave with a more-regal bearing, yes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk! We ought be forming strong alliances, not at each other's throats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Why are we fighting, blast? You truly are one of us to get such a rise out of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Mustn't keep all our anger bottled up, yet always have some in reserve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are found! Such a relief our destinies are safely back in their hands. I was beginning to fear the course of our futures would forever be ours to decide. Perhaps once we're older, and more responsible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: A new age is to dawn soon enough, is that it? That much, the Three Fates guarantee, but otherwise we can do as we please! Well, fine. If mortals think they can get on without our intervention, I should like to see them try!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: How fare you in your task of late, my dear? Much better than we fare up here, I hope. Oh, all of us are fine, except... being besieged does mean we're stuck in closer quarters than I'd like. There's not a moment's peace!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Take it from me, my dear, don't listen to your heart. I know it's difficult, seeing as the blasted thing tends to be very loud. But if you heed it, it'll lead you straight to your mistakes more often than not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My word, girl. To have delivered a groveling, impotent Chronos right into our hands, after having utterly destroyed Typhon... one astonishing feat after another, even for a goddess of your pedigree. How can we ever repay you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't see why you keep heading this way, when it's your Underworld that seems to need more help. The mountain's fine! Well mostly. Other than these remnant forces of the Titan ruffling our feathers still...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Need I remind you that I'm not particularly fond of those who turn their backs on me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: How disappointing yet how typical this is! Now it's my turn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Whom do you take me for? You think I shall simply roll over and accept this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you belong here on Olympus after all, showing such rudeness to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Beware of angering me thus, my dear. You'll find I have an excellent memory.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You would be wise not to forget yourself with me. Here! Let me remind you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Deep down, I value dissenting voices and opinions, but outwardly... no.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We must maintain decorum; neither can we tolerate such insubordination.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: If you insist on siding against me, then I insist on making you regret it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We goddesses truly ought to be more committed to each other than this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My husband is very accustomed to getting his way; you need not indulge him so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: This swarthy son of mine is more a draw to you than I? Hahaha!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: When next my Husband and I have a little disagreement, side with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: My warmongering son is likely to blame for all this, yet you choose him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: What my sister may lack for youth she makes up for with wisdom, is that it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Each night presents to us a new beginning, isn't that so, my dear? And on this night, I shall accompany you from the very start... until the bitter end.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Underworld has long since fallen, but our Olympus yet stands; you're wise to head this way, where you can make a real difference, here and now. As I shall momentarily!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: A single, capable combatant striking our enemies from their exposed flanks may be just what we require to drive them back. Looks like you're up for the job tonight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Your descent into the Underworld has only begun, but I would join you from the very first steps. I'd far prefer it to all my other obligations put together, let me tell you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: You came closer than ever to our mountaintop! I hoped, perhaps, that we could meet at last, but... we've more pressing matters to attend to now, the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We saw reports suggesting that you made it to the lowest depths! I thought perhaps you had prevailed... though, not yet, it seems. A sign of progress, nonetheless! Press on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Word here is you somehow got the old wretch last night! Splendid if so. You'd think Time itself would know when to get out of the younger generation's way. But if Chronos requires more convincing, so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: However satisfying it must be to cut Chronos down to size, it's not reason enough. No, you and that Witch of the Crossroads must be brewing up some greater plan...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Even Titans and gods are quite vulnerable creatures, deep down. For we are all driven by a most-delicate instrument, as liable to stir our spirits or drive us to despair, as my dear Aphrodite knows full well.

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Come now, my Queen! The heart's the strongest part we've got and tougher than it seems! We've withstood a bit upon this mountaintop, but we can take much more. Especially when our hearts all beat as one!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I fear for the younger generation. What can you possibly achieve when you're born with our worst qualities and not merely our best? Take good Apollo here... just like his father.

dialogueLines.speaker: ApolloUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Now, Stepmother, don't all we gods aspire to your husband's excellence? I do appreciate the kind comparison! My fair cousin likewise surely has her father's finest traits!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: At least Lord Ares is back to where it's easier for me to keep an eye on him. Come, my son, let's make ourselves somewhat presentable? For the benefit of my niece there.

dialogueLines.speaker: AresUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: It shall be as you wish, O Queen. I'm honored that you sought me out, and trust our kin shall be most grateful to you in turn, as the vessel of the sum of our wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Much as your father was custodian of all the dead, so are we responsible for the rest of the world! The hour has grown dark, yes, but... we have the power to restore. Thanks be to my good sister Demeter...

dialogueLines.speaker: DemeterUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: The time for restoration always comes as does the spring. But we're amidst a long winter, without an end in sight. We need concern ourselves with naught else, O Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: I know full well we cannot stand on ceremony whilst our entire way of life is under threat! Yet surely my dear son Hephaestus here need not be so uncouth at every opportunity?

dialogueLines.speaker: HephaestusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Not like you ever taught me proper manners, Mum. Fortunately, I did learn a couple things even without having a role model like you or Dad around to set me straight! Just watch. And witchie, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, this is a remarkable development, my dear! Hephaestus has emerged, however briefly from his forge. I asked my son to greet his cousin properly, but didn't think he would.

dialogueLines.speaker: HephaestusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh come on, Mum, you didn't think I'd end up doing much of anything. I know my best ain't always good enough, but then again, whose is? Now, can I get back to my work?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: We goddesses can be too proud to beg for aid! I'd never stoop to it myself. Why bother, when my hard-headed sister Hestia shall insist on intervening, anyway? And at this very moment whilst I speak!

dialogueLines.speaker: HestiaUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Because, respectfully, you just don't know what's good for you, O Queen! Sometimes it's ones can't bring themselves to ask for help that need it most! Now why don't we set a proper example for the latest unceremonious addition to our family?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: A shame you never knew your father, girl... but at least now you know his good brother Poseidon, here! Imagine this one, but without the foolish grin, nor the inappropriate remarks, with frankly better hair, in finer physical form.

dialogueLines.speaker: PoseidonUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahaha! See how strong a family we are, my niece?! For even in the face of such catastrophe as we have all endured, the Queen herself is able to make such humorous remarks! At my expense. But still!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah! Look, my dear! It's my beloved husband. The distinguished King of the Olympians, who shall lead us all out of this mess. How goes it, Son of Chronos, any news?

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha! Come now, Hera dear, for all your jesting with me, it can send the wrong impression to one unaccustomed to your dry and clever wit! You see that, Melinoë? Humor is the bond that makes a marriage strong!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hera_01
dialogueLines.text: Our family's unity does not come cheap. We all must work together to ensure the bonds connecting us don't fray, or split entirely as was the case with Chronos. My Lord Husband knows this better than anyone, don't you, Zeus?

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: You're quite right, Dear, that marriage can most certainly begin to feel like work! It was so easy for us in the early days, but our responsibilities have grown since, haven't they?
